items.title: Geopolitics and Tech Woes Put Traders on Edge Before Weekend
items.author: Alexander Nicholson
items.content: <article>Even as US stock futures point to tentative gains on Wall Street, AI spending plans and Middle East tensions give plenty to worry about</article>

items.title: Dollar Set for Best Week in a Month as Haven Flows Offer Support
items.author: Vassilis Karamanis
items.content: <article>The dollar is heading for its best week in a month as investors return to the traditional haven amid heightened geopolitical tensions.</article>

items.title: Gulf States Set for Debt Binge to Build Routes Avoiding Hormuz
items.author: Abhinav Ramnarayan
items.content: <article>Traders are preparing for a spike in borrowing from Persian Gulf nations as the region’s oil exporters seek financing to build costly bypasses around the Strait of Hormuz.</article>

items.title: Euronext Mulls Setting Up a Private Market to Rival the UK’s
items.author: Charles Capel, Pablo Mayo Cerqueiro and Laura Noonan
items.content: <article>Euronext NV is considering creating a market for private securities, potentially ramping up competition with the London Stock Exchange Group Plc as bourses seek to offer investors access to fast-growing private companies.</article>

items.title: BofA’s Raedler Says Investors Are Ignoring Stock-Market Risks
items.author: Matt Clinch and Anna Edwards
items.content: <article>Stock markets are pricing in a scenario where everything goes right, leaving investors uncompensated for mounting risks, according to Sebastian Raedler, head of European equity strategy at Bank of America Corp.</article>

items.title: Mauritius Tax Wave Threatens to Erode Its Banks’ Profitability
items.author: Adelaide Changole and Kamlesh Bhuckory
items.content: <article>A raft of new corporate taxes that could erode banks’ profitability is testing Mauritius’s long-standing status as a low-tax gateway for cross-border capital, according to Moody’s Ratings.</article>

items.title: PIC: Why South Africa’s Biggest Fund Manager Is in Turmoil
items.author: Alexander Parker and Mike Cohen
items.content: <article>The South African firm that oversees state workers’ pensions has been plunged into a fresh crisis triggered by the suspension of its chief executive and the resignation of its chairman.</article>

items.title: Victoria Plc Slumps to £326 Million Loss as Debt Deal Bites
items.author: Tuhin Kar
items.content: <article>Victoria Plc reported a wider annual loss as the troubled British carpetmaker counted the cost of its recent debt refinancing.</article>

items.title: Zara’s Ortega Buys Paris Office Complex for About €800 Million
items.author: Clara Hernanz Lizarraga
items.content: <article>Zara billionaire Amancio Ortega bought a business center in Paris from Invesco Ltd., in what is his biggest transaction in Europe.</article>

items.title: How Franchise Restaurants Opened the Door to the Gig Economy
items.author: Joe Weisenthal and Tracy Alloway
items.content: The ruthless labor history of your favorite fast food joint.

items.title: Odd Lots: How Franchising Led to the Gig Economy (Podcast)
items.author: Joe Weisenthal and Tracy Alloway
items.content: <article>When the fast food industry began booming in the 1950s, it did so via a new business model known as the franchise. This model allowed independent operators to license trademarks from a business like McDonald’s or Dunkin Donuts, and it soon spread across the country, with huge consequences for how Americans are employed. Legal battles fought by franchises eventually opened the door to what’s now known as the gig economy, allowing Uber drivers to be treated in much the same way as the operator of </article>

items.title: Oil falls back below $100 as Trump weighs ‘massive attack’
items.content: <article>Middle East tensions have risen after Red Sea attacks by Houthi militants threatened to squeeze global supplies further</article>

items.title: Russian online retailer Wildberries becomes target for Ukraine’s drones
items.content: <article>Strikes on Wildberries have killed nine people and left small sellers facing millions of roubles in losses </article>

items.title: US hits 60 countries with new duties as Trump rebuilds tariff wall
items.content: <article>Washington moves to reimpose 10 per cent duties after previous blanket levies were struck down by Supreme Court</article>

items.title: Tanker Exits Red Sea Dark as China Ship Heads Toward Strait
items.author: Weilun Soon
items.content: <article>A Greek tanker carrying Saudi crude exited the Red Sea with its transponder off, while another Chinese vessel and Russian cargoes headed toward the Bab el-Mandeb strait, after Houthi attacks heightened risks.</article>

items.title: Dangote Prefers London Over Dubai for Listing of Cement Unit
items.author: Jennifer Zabasajja
items.content: <article>Dangote Cement Plc prefers to list its cement business in London because it will be quicker to sell shares on the exchange in the UK than in Dubai, according to Mariya Dangote, a board member in the cement business.</article>

items.title: Global Bonds Are Reeling as Oil Surge Renews Inflation Threat
items.author: Greg Ritchie and Cameron Fozi
items.content: <article>Global bonds are being pummeled by the latest resurgence in energy prices, delivering losses to investors who bet the worst of this year’s rout was over and teeing up credibility tests for central bankers.</article>

items.title: Trump to Take Up Manila’s Concerns With Xi, Philippines Says
items.author: Ditas B Lopez
items.content: <article>US President Donald Trump assured Philippine leader Ferdinand Marcos Jr. that he would take up Manila’s concerns when he meets with Chinese President Xi Jinping, the Southeast Asian nation said.</article>

items.title: Modi Seeks to Calm Anger as India Student Protests Widen
items.author: Ruchi Bhatia and Aryan Gupta
items.content: <article>Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi made a fresh bid to calm mounting anger over examination paper leaks as students continued nationwide protests on Friday, with demands growing for the education minister to step down.</article>

items.content: <article>Chipmaker’s revenue jumped 25% in the second quarter</article>

items.title: Goldman, BofA See 2024 Flashback in China Before Politburo Meets
items.author: Bloomberg News
items.content: <article>As the calendar turned to July, Chinese retail sales were decelerating fast, the stock market was in the doldrums and investment unexpectedly turned for the worse. Public spending shrank as local governments struggled with financing.</article>

items.title: Japan Is Whipsawing Wall Street’s CLO Machine, Study Shows
items.author: Scott Carpenter
items.content: <article>The $1.3 trillion US CLO market is taking its cues from Tokyo as much as Wall Street.</article>

items.title: Stocks Get Some Relief as Brent Slips Below $100: Markets Wrap
items.author: Andre Janse van Vuuren and Sagarika Jaisinghani
items.content: <article>US stocks are finding some respite after posting their biggest drop this month as Brent crude eased below $100 a barrel and bond yields halted their ascent.</article>

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 24
items.author: Grant Smith and Paul Burkhardt
items.content: <article>Brent crude retreated after hitting $100 a barrel in the previous session on concern over widening risks to Middle East supplies, as US President Donald Trump’s revived trade tariffs posed economic headwinds.</article>
